name: literature-review
tagline: Guides systematic literature reviews — multi-database search, screening, citation verification, and PDF/figure generation.
verdict: Detailed, well-structured research workflow, but its Dependencies section pipes a curl download straight into bash (unverified installer) and the pipeline leans on several unaudited third-party skills/services (parallel-cli, gget, OpenRouter) — a real supply-chain risk for installers who follow the setup steps literally. Also overlong (711 lines) with a dupl

highlights.summary: Line 670: `curl -fsSL https://parallel.ai/install.sh | bash` — downloads and executes a remote script directly into bash with no checksum/signature verification.

Phase 2/Citation Chaining instruct fetching full paper content via `parallel-cli extract` (lines 115-118, 406-409) and treating it as review material, with no guidance to treat fetched external text a

required_environment_variables (line 6) prompts the user to supply an OPENROUTER_API_KEY; marked optional and used for the skill's own calls, but it is still a live-secret request bundled into the fro
